title: SPARQL DISTINCT gives duplicates in Virtuoso
description:
There is a problem with distinct when there are floats involved.
Here it is the error commented and reproduced.
http://stackoverflow.com/questions/38371049/sparql-distinct-gives-duplicates-in-virtuoso
poc:
PREFIX opp: <http://schema.oceanproteinportal.org/v2/>
PREFIX sosa: <http://www.w3.org/ns/sosa/>
SELECT DISTINCT ?label ?depth ?station
WHERE {
  <urn:opp:dataset:metzyme-0.2> opp:storesResultsForSample ?sample .
    ?sample rdfs:label ?label .
    FILTER REGEX(?label, "CID") .
    ?sample sosa:isSampleOf ?feature .
    ?feature opp:depth ?depth .
    ?feature opp:inVicinityOfStation ?stationInfo .
    ?stationInfo opp:stationName ?station .
}
ORDER BY ?label

PREFIX opp: <http://schema.oceanproteinportal.org/v2/>
PREFIX sosa: <http://www.w3.org/ns/sosa/>
SELECT DISTINCT ?label STR(?depth) as ?depth_as_string ?station
WHERE {
  <urn:opp:dataset:metzyme-0.2> opp:storesResultsForSample ?sample .
    ?sample rdfs:label ?label .
    FILTER REGEX(?label, "CID") .
    ?sample sosa:isSampleOf ?feature .
    ?feature opp:depth ?depth .
    ?feature opp:inVicinityOfStation ?stationInfo .
    ?stationInfo opp:stationName ?station .
}
ORDER BY ?label
key_bug_features: SPARQL DISTINCT with float values causes duplicates
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE sample_data (id INT PRIMARY KEY, label VARCHAR(255), depth FLOAT, station VARCHAR(255));
INSERT INTO sample_data (id, label, depth, station) VALUES (1, 'CID_1', 10.5, 'Station_A');
INSERT INTO sample_data (id, label, depth, station) VALUES (2, 'CID_1', 10.5, 'Station_B');
INSERT INTO sample_data (id, label, depth, station) VALUES (3, 'CID_2', 20.0, 'Station_A');
SELECT DISTINCT label, CAST(depth AS VARCHAR) AS depth_as_string, station FROM sample_data WHERE label LIKE 'CID%';
